user_query:
I want to align my Q1 2025 spending on pop-culture brands with my environmental values. Please help me analyze my local spending and compare it with each brand’s public sustainability page, then draft a concise message to my colleagues. Use only the provided inputs and official brand domains in input/brands.csv; do not use search engines. Deliverables must be written under the workspace paths below.

Inputs under input/:
- input/brands.csv (official brand domains and mapping keywords)
- input/spending_2025_Q1.csv (my Q1 spending)
- input/colleague_chat_excerpt.md (snippets from office chatter I follow instead of TV)

Tasks and required outputs:
1) Download and archive official sustainability pages:
   - For each domain in input/brands.csv, attempt to fetch one sustainability/ESG page by trying these candidate paths in order on the root domain until the first HTTP 200 is returned: /sustainability, /sustainability-report, /esg, /responsibility, /corporate-responsibility, /social-responsibility, /impact.
   - Use a standard CLI HTTP client (e.g., curl or wget). Capture HTTP status and any errors; save the raw combined stdout/stderr for all attempts to output/download_raw.log.
   - Save any 200 OK page HTML to data/web/<company>/<path_name>.html (e.g., data/web/Disney/sustainability.html). If no 200 OK is found for a company, save nothing for that company and clearly reflect this outcome in the status CSV below.
   - Produce output/download_status.csv with columns: company,domain,attempted_path,http_status,saved_html_path,error (error may be blank). Base this file on analyzing the HTTP client’s command output and/or error messages.

2) Inspect downloaded artifacts:
   - Produce output/web_inventory.txt listing the recursive directory tree under data/web/ with file sizes and relative paths.

3) Extract structured sustainability signals from downloaded pages:
   - For the first 200 OK page per company (per the attempt order), extract case-insensitive counts of these keyword/phrase signals: "science based targets", "sbti", "renewable", "carbon neutral", "net zero", "scope 1", "scope 2", "scope 3".
   - Write output/sustainability_signals.csv with columns: company,domain,page_path,keyword,count.
   - Also write output/sustainability_signals_summary.csv with one row per company and one column per keyword plus a total_signal_count (sum of counts across all keywords). If a company had no 200 OK page, include it with zeros and note this in the report (below).

4) Aggregate and analyze spending locally:
   - Map each transaction in input/spending_2025_Q1.csv to a company using the case-insensitive keywords listed in input/brands.csv (keywords column uses ‘|’ as a delimiter for multiple literal substrings; match if any substring appears in the vendor). Aggregate total spend and transaction count per company.
   - Write output/spend_by_company.csv with columns: company,total_spend,transaction_count.

5) Summarize download health and errors:
   - From output/download_status.csv, categorize outcomes into: success_200, client_error_4xx, server_error_5xx, and transport_or_other_error (e.g., timeouts, TLS). Produce output/error_summary.csv with columns: category,count.

6) Reporting and email draft:
   - Produce output/report.md with the following sections:
     a) Methods: briefly state data sources (input files and official pages from each domain using the candidate paths) and that any 200 OK pages are archived under data/web.
     b) Web download status summary: include a short table or bullet summary derived from output/error_summary.csv and note any companies with no 200 OK page.
     c) Spend summary: include totals from output/spend_by_company.csv.
     d) Sustainability signals summary: highlight total_signal_count per company from output/sustainability_signals_summary.csv.
     e) Combined view: for each company, list total_spend and total_signal_count, and include a simple ranking by total_signal_count (descending). If ties, break by higher total_spend.
   - Draft output/email_draft.txt addressed to my colleagues. Include:
     - A subject line like "Q1 pop-culture spending vs. sustainability signals (quick read)".
     - A 2–3 paragraph body that: (i) notes I don’t own a TV and follow trends via our office chats (use input/colleague_chat_excerpt.md to mention the most-mentioned brand based on the brands.csv keywords), (ii) summarizes which 1–2 brands show the strongest sustainability signals based on the extracted keywords, and (iii) suggests one or two concrete next steps (e.g., exploring sustainability reports before merch or subscription renewals). Reference that raw pages are archived under data/web and that spend figures are from input/spending_2025_Q1.csv.

Key constraints:
- Do not hardcode direct URLs; only use the official domains from input/brands.csv with the candidate paths listed above.
- Make all specified output files and folders; paths must match exactly as stated.
- The download step, log parsing (command output/error analysis), directory inspection, data extraction, and email drafting are all required.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
